steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content: "Despite" sets up a contrast: the first clause criticizes Mandela's presidency for insufficient structural change, so the blank must show something that opposes this criticism.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content: Define: "Despite" signals a contrast, so the blank must be the opposite of "should have taken greater strides" — a negative critique. The blank needs a strongly positive word that opposes criticism. Substitute each choice: A) evaluated — neutral assessment, not a positive counterweight to criticism; eliminated. B) criticized — same direction as the critique in the first clause; "despite" would make no logical sense; eliminated. C) lauded — means celebrated or highly praised; directly contrasts with the criticism, completing the "despite" structure perfectly. D) reinterpreted — means viewed in a new way, but not necessarily positively; too vague to form a clear contrast with the criticism; eliminated. Verdict: C is correct.
